series_metadata.series_description: Includes application materials and supporting documentation for individual applications, including, but not limited to, eligibility information, inspections, payments, denials, appeals, and other documents filed with and related to the applicant.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format, then destroy. If converted to electronic/digital format, paper records may be destroyed after verification of successful conversion. ; Electronic: FEDERAL PROGRAMS: Retain three (3) years after closeout of file, or three (3) years after date of denial for unfunded/unsuccessful applications, then destroy. NON-FEDERAL PROGRAMS: Retain two (2) years after closeout of file, or (2) years after date of denial for unfunded/unsuccessful applications, then destroy. Statutory or regulatory provisions applicable to certain programs may affect retention of related records. In such cases, the statutory or regulatory provisions shall take precedence over the retention language set forth in this schedule. Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed., Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Erase
